blue.$description: Primary brand blue. SuperDoc's core identity color.

50.$description: Lightest blue tint. Use for subtle backgrounds and hover states on light surfaces.

100.$description: Very light blue. Use for selected/active backgrounds, badges, and highlights.

200.$description: Light blue. Use for borders on active elements and secondary indicators.

300.$description: Medium-light blue. Use for icons and secondary text on dark backgrounds.

400.$description: Medium blue. Use for links and interactive elements on hover.

500.$description: Primary brand blue. The canonical SuperDoc blue. Use for primary buttons, active states, and key brand moments.

600.$description: Dark blue. Use for primary button hover states and pressed states.

700.$description: Darker blue. Use for focused states and high-contrast text on light backgrounds.

800.$description: Very dark blue. Use for headings and emphasis text.

900.$description: Deepest blue. Use sparingly for maximum contrast.

gray.$description: Neutral grays for UI chrome, borders, and text.

50.$description: Near-white. Use for subtle background differentiation (e.g., page canvas).

100.$description: Light gray background. Use for cards, sidebars, and secondary surfaces.

200.$description: Slightly darker surface. Use for hover backgrounds on light surfaces.

300.$description: Border-light. Use for subtle dividers and inactive borders.

400.$description: Standard border gray. Use for input borders, dividers, and separators.

500.$description: Mid gray. Use for placeholder text and disabled borders.

600.$description: Muted text. Use for secondary labels and metadata.

700.$description: Secondary text. Use for descriptions and supporting content.

800.$description: Primary text (secondary). Use for body text in some contexts.

900.$description: Primary text. Use for headings and body text.

500.$description: Primary error red. Use for error messages, destructive buttons, and validation states.
Primary green. Use for success states and track-changes insertions.
Deletion red. Use for track-changes deletions and strikethrough highlights.

white.$description: Pure white. Primary background for document pages and content areas.

black.$description: Pure black. Use for document text rendering.
